name: Optimizing sugaar HTTP and network stack

description: Baseline: HTTP/network stack benchmarks with quiet logging

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement of current HTTP/network stack performance
asi.setup: Custom quiet benchmarks to eliminate log noise

description: Pre-compute middleware chain in App to eliminate per-request closure allocations

asi.hypothesis: Pre-computing the middleware chain eliminates len(mws) closure allocations per request
asi.impact: route_allocs reduced by 10 (22%), route_ns reduced by 27ns (2.6%)
asi.next: Continue with context.WithValue + Request.WithContext allocation elimination

description: Reuse statusWriter via Context field and use json.Marshal instead of json.NewEncoder

asi.hypothesis: Reusing statusWriter eliminates 1 alloc per request; json.Marshal avoids encoder alloc
asi.next: Eliminate context.WithValue + Request.WithContext allocations via ctxWriter approach

description: Store request ID directly in Context field and use unsafe.String for hex encoding to eliminate map allocation

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating Context.Set map allocation for request ID and using stack-allocated hex encoding reduces allocs
asi.next: Optimize Hub.Publish subscriber slice pooling or reduce log.Warn allocations

description: Use slog.LogAttrs instead of log.Info in requestLogMiddleware to avoid variadic slice allocation

asi.hypothesis: slog.LogAttrs with slog.Attr values avoids the []any slice allocation from variadic log.Info
asi.next: Further reduce route allocations: context.WithValue + Request.WithContext are 15% of remaining allocs; optimize SSE writes or Hub fanout

description: Use sync.Map keyed by *http.Request instead of Request.WithContext + context.WithValue to eliminate 2 allocations per request

asi.warning: sync.Map may have contention overhead under high concurrency; monitor if hub latency degrades
asi.next: Optimize Hub.Publish subscriber slice or explore faster map for reqCtxMap

description: Use slog.LogAttrs instead of log.Warn in Hub.Publish to eliminate per-drop allocation

asi.hypothesis: slog.LogAttrs with a single slog.String avoids the []any slice allocation from variadic log.Warn
asi.next: Hub is now very lean. Focus back on route_ns: optimize json.Marshal or reduce MIMEHeader.Set allocations

description: Reverted SSE buffer pool due to benchmark noise; confirming baseline after revert

asi.hypothesis: Benchmark environment is noisy; ns/op varies widely but allocs are stable at 22
asi.rollback_reason: Environmental noise makes ns/op unreliable; allocation count is the stable signal
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on allocation-reducing optimizations and accept that ns/op will have variance

description: Use unsafe.Slice to convert string to []byte without copying in Context.String, saving 1 alloc per call

asi.hypothesis: unsafe.Slice + unsafe.StringData avoids io.WriteString's []byte allocation for writers without WriteString
asi.note: ns/op noisy due to system load; allocation reduction is the reliable signal

description: Replace sync.Map with mutex-guarded map to eliminate per-request entry node allocation

asi.hypothesis: sync.Map entry node allocation is ~1 alloc per request; mutex+map avoids this
asi.risk: Global mutex may contend under high concurrency; consider sharding if profiles show lock contention

description: Eliminate subscriber slice allocation in Hub.Publish by sending directly while holding the lock

asi.hypothesis: Sending directly while holding lock eliminates make([]*Subscription) alloc
asi.risk: Holding lock during send may increase contention with Subscribe/unsubscribe for very large fanouts

description: Cache Content-Type header value slices to avoid per-request []string allocation in Header.Set

asi.hypothesis: Header.Set allocates []string{value} on every call; reusing global slices avoids this
asi.risk: Shared slices are safe because append with cap=1 always reallocates; no standard library code modifies slice backing arrays in place

description: Inline error handler into chained middleware to remove if-check in ServeHTTP

asi.hypothesis: Removing if err != nil in ServeHTTP might save a branch
asi.impact: No change in allocs or ns/op; branch predictor likely already handles this
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on larger wins like reducing json.Marshal allocs or map literal allocations
